source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/05_Blue (G1417MCW 외 7종)/▶ 시험소/06. Usability 보완_250103/06. 제품 회로도/PMIC(WIFI).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Circuit schematic pages for PCIe M.2 WiFi and related USB/OLED interface circuitry dated Monday, July 15, 2024.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports product circuit diagram documentation in an RA/test-lab source folder.
keyPoints: Title block identifies the schematic as PCIe.M2(WiFi).
PCIe WiFi sections specify 85Ω impedance for TX, RX, and CLK signals.
USB3.0 layout note specifies 90ohm impedance and equal USB signal length.
OLED I2C address is listed as 0x3C.

concepts.name: USB3.0 layout impedance
concepts.evidence: USB3.0 Layout: 90ohm, USB signals length equal

evidence.claim: The document title block identifies the drawing as PCIe.M2(WiFi).
evidence.evidenceText: Title Size Document Number R e v Date: Sheet o f <Doc> a PCIe.M2(WiFi) A4 1 1 Monday, July 15, 2024

evidence.claim: The document date shown in the title block is Monday, July 15, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Date: Sheet o f <Doc> a PCIe.M2(WiFi) A4 1 1 Monday, July 15, 2024

evidence.claim: The PCIe M.2 WiFi section specifies 85Ω impedance for TX, RX, and CLK.
The USB3.0 layout note specifies 90ohm impedance and equal USB signal length.
evidence.evidenceText: USB3.0 Layout: 90ohm, USB signals length equal

evidence.claim: The OLED I2C address is listed as 0x3C.
A second PCIe WiFi section uses PCIE#B signals and WF_ENABLE_SIG_B.
evidence.evidenceText: PCIE#B_CLK_P_C PCIE#B_RESET_B PCIE#B_RX0_P PCIE#B_RX0_N PCIE#B_TX0_P PCIE#B_TX0_N PCIE#B_CLK_N_C WF_ENABLE_SIG_B

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is general.
No product model is stated in the schematic text; productSignal is Unclassified.
Revision is only clearly shown as 'a' on the PCIe.M2(WiFi) title block; other repeated title blocks contain placeholder text such as <RevCo and <Title>.
Source appears to be OCR or text extraction from schematics, with repeated title-block fragments and fragmented pin text.
No regulatory standards are cited in the source text.
